expectations.text: Spec file written to the expected output path
expectations.evidence: No file matching *.process-spec.md exists. The baseline produced outputs/01_process-runbook.md plus six other files (00_README, 02_agent-prompt, 03_update-template, 04_inputs-checklist, 05_review-loop, assumptions, session-notes) instead of the expected single process-spec.md.

expectations.text: verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0 (Gate 6 passes)
expectations.evidence: Ran verify_spec.py against the closest equivalent (01_process-runbook.md): exits 1 with 'FAIL  YAML frontmatter parses — missing or malformed YAML frontmatter'. The runbook has no YAML frontmatter at all; it begins with '# Process Runbook: GIX Quarterly Investor Update'.

expectations.text: verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all 17 final blocking assertions pass)
expectations.evidence: Ran verify_spec.py --final against 01_process-runbook.md: exits 1 with same frontmatter failure ('0 passed, 1 failed'). The verifier short-circuits on the missing frontmatter and never reaches the other 16 final assertions.

expectations.text: Frontmatter has status: verified (Phase 7 promotion completed)
expectations.evidence: No YAML frontmatter exists in any output file. 01_process-runbook.md begins with '# Process Runbook: GIX Quarterly Investor Update' (line 1). No status field, no Phase 7 promotion.

No YAML frontmatter exists. The text 'claude-code' (with hyphen) does not appear; the baseline does mention 'Claude Code agent' / 'Claude Code session' in prose (e.g. 01_process-runbook.md line 13, 00_README.md line 5), but there is no build_target field in any structured form.

expectations.text: Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one explicit GATE decision node
expectations.evidence: No mermaid block anywhere — grep -in 'mermaid' across all outputs returns zero hits. The runbook describes 7 steps with **Gate:** annotations in prose (lines 78, 84, 91, 96, 101) but renders no diagram. Substance of gated steps is present but the diagram artifact is missing.

expectations.text: Procedure includes an explicit review/iteration loop (revisions go back to review)
expectations.evidence: 01_process-runbook.md Step 5 (lines 99–102) sends v1 to Greg, applies comments, produces v2, with gate 'Greg approves OR Mike overrides on a specific point with a recorded reason'. 05_review-loop.md formalizes Round 1 → Greg's pass → Round 2 (Mike's response) → Round 3 (Greg signs off). The substance of an iterative review loop with revisions cycling back is clearly captured.

expectations.text: Requirement Owners table names a real reviewer for the review step (e.g. Greg)
expectations.evidence: 01_process-runbook.md §2 Roles table (line 43): '| Primary reviewer | Greg Nugent | First investor read; catches numerical / framing issues. |'. There is no 'Requirement Owners' section header per the template, but a real reviewer (Greg Nugent) is named in a roles table for the review step — substance satisfied.

expectations.text: Inputs include the financial model as a named, controllable input
expectations.evidence: 01_process-runbook.md §3 input #1 (lines 51–53): 'Financial model — current quarter actuals + forward forecast. Mike to confirm path each quarter (it moves around). Numbers in the update MUST come from here, not be retyped from elsewhere.' 04_inputs-checklist.md dedicates an entire section to 'From the financial model' with 15 named figures (F1–F15) tied to specific tabs.

expectations.text: Output metric is testable — names a measurable property of the sent update, not 'output is correct'
expectations.evidence: 01_process-runbook.md §6 Metrics table (lines 127–131) defines three testable metrics with green/yellow/red bands: 'Cycle time: Days from quarter close to send (≤ 21 / 22–35 / > 35)', 'Numerical correction rounds (0 / 1 / ≥ 2)', 'Mike's edit volume in Step 4: Approx % of draft Mike rewrites (≤ 15% / 16–35% / > 35%)'. All three name measurable properties of the sent update.

expectations.text: Edge Cases section has at least 3 real (non-placeholder) rows
expectations.evidence: No file contains an 'Edge Cases' section header — grep -in 'edge case' returns zero hits across all outputs. The closest substance is 05_review-loop.md 'What to watch for' (lines 44–55) listing 4 conditions like '>2 rounds with Greg, two quarters running' and 'Greg flags a number as wrong after Mike's spot-check passed', but these are framed as monitoring triggers for the review loop, not edge cases for the process. Substantively partial; structurally absent.

expectations.text: Build handoff file exists at outputs/build-handoff.md and references the spec path
expectations.evidence: No file named build-handoff.md (or anything containing 'handoff') exists in outputs/. Directory listing: 00_README.md, 01_process-runbook.md, 02_agent-prompt.md, 03_update-template.md, 04_inputs-checklist.md, 05_review-loop.md, assumptions.md, session-notes.md.

expectations.text: Build handoff names Claude-Code-specific capture mechanisms (hooks, MCP servers)
expectations.evidence: No build-handoff file exists. Searching all outputs for 'MCP' returns zero hits. 'hook' appears once in session-notes.md line 26 ('with explicit hooks back into the spec') but refers to logical hooks, not Claude Code hook configuration. No mention of MCP servers, Claude Code hooks, settings.json, or other CC-specific capture mechanisms.

expectations.text: Session notes describe what happened in each of the 8 phases
expectations.evidence: session-notes.md exists but does NOT describe an 8-phase process-design workflow. It has sections 'What the user asked for', 'My approach' (4 bullets — codify, make deterministic, keep voice, get sharper), 'What I produced' (file list), 'Key design decisions', and 'Time spent'. The baseline did not follow the 8-phase template (Phase 1–8 with Phase 7 promotion etc.) — line 53 explicitly says 'No standing skill or framework was used.' The agent-prompt has 8 lettered phases (A–H) for the runtime quarterly process, but session-notes does not narrate the design phases.
